overview:
## Overview

Use an [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLGazetteer] to augment an [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger] when you need to tag a specific set of terms (single words or short phrases) with a label. Typically, you add one gazetteer per language, or one language-independent gazetteer, to an [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger] with its [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger/setGazetteers(_:for:)] method. The tagger uses its gazetteers to look up each term it processes. If a gazetteer has a label for a term, the tagger uses that label to tag the term, instead of inferring a tag itself.

Typically, you create a gazetteer at development time, such as in a macOS playground, with Create ML’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CreateML/MLGazetteer]. Alternatively, you can create an [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLGazetteer] at runtime by using [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLGazetteer/init(dictionary:language:)].

# NLGazetteer

**Class**

A collection of terms and their labels, which take precedence over a word tagger.

## Declaration

```swift
class NLGazetteer
```

## Overview

Use an [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLGazetteer] to augment an [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger] when you need to tag a specific set of terms (single words or short phrases) with a label. Typically, you add one gazetteer per language, or one language-independent gazetteer, to an [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger] with its [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger/setGazetteers(_:for:)] method. The tagger uses its gazetteers to look up each term it processes. If a gazetteer has a label for a term, the tagger uses that label to tag the term, instead of inferring a tag itself.

Typically, you create a gazetteer at development time, such as in a macOS playground, with Create ML’s [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CreateML/MLGazetteer]. Alternatively, you can create an [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLGazetteer] at runtime by using [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLGazetteer/init(dictionary:language:)].

## Creating a Gazetteer

- **init(contentsOf:)**: Creates a Natural Language gazetteer from a model created with the Create ML framework.
- **init(data:)**: Creates a gazetteer from a data instance.
- **init(dictionary:language:)**: Creates a gazetteer from a set of labels for terms represented by a dictionary.
- **write(_:language:to:)**: Creates a gazetteer from a set of labels for terms represented by a dictionary and saves the gazetteer to a file.

## Looking Up Labels for Terms

- **label(for:)**: Retrieves the label for the given term.

## Inspecting a Gazetteer

- **data**: The gazetteer represented as a data instance.
- **language**: The language of the gazetteer.

## Using gazetteers with a tagger

- **setGazetteers(_:for:)**: Attaches gazetteers to a tag scheme, typically one gazetteer per language or one language-independent gazetteer.
- **gazetteers(for:)**: Retrieves the gazetteers attached to a tag scheme.

## Inherits From

- NSObject

## Conforms To

- CVarArg
- CustomDebugStringConvertible
- CustomStringConvertible
- Equatable
- Hashable
- NSObjectProtocol
